channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Can't get a prescription renewed? Here's how to cope with prior authorizations
item.description: These health care hurdles can stand in the way of getting treatment your doctor says you need. Here's what to know about how to deal with them.

item.title: Virtual reality rehabilitation is safe and feasible for upper extremity recovery after subacute ischemic stroke
item.description: 1. Non-immersive virtual reality rehabilitation was feasible and safe but did not demonstrate superiority over equally intensive conventional physioth...

item.title: Residents With Children Are at a Financial Breaking Point
item.description: As childcare costs soar and pay lags behind inflation, residents are forced into impossible choices about family and training. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Younger Women Face Increase in Heart Disease Risk
item.description: A new AHA scientific statement warns that cardiovascular disease is rising fastest among younger women, with stroke rates in those aged 20-44 years pr...

item.title: MHRA Clears Brensocatib for Bronchiectasis
item.description: The MHRA has licensed brensocatib as the first disease-specific therapy for non-cystic fibrosis bronchiectasis in patients with recurrent exacerbation...

item.title: Cigarette smoke accelerates eye aging via epigenetic changes, study finds
item.description: Through a series of experiments supported by the National Institutes of Health, Johns Hopkins Medicine (JHM) researchers say they have advanced unders...

item.title: Designing calmer workplaces: How pine scent and wood visuals may aid recovery
item.description: The scent of wood and visual wood elements reduce stress and significantly enhance recovery and mood indoors. The well-being effects of virtual wooden...

item.title: Navigating the hype and hope of psychedelic medicine [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Psychiatrist, internist, and addiction medicine specialist Muhamad Al...

item.title: Music may not boost focus or mood during exercise
item.description: Music is commonly used during exercise and is often assumed to improve focus, mood, or mental performance. A new systematic review and meta-analysis l...

item.title: CKD Linked to Higher Risk for Workforce Detachment
item.description: Chronic kidney disease is associated with a significantly increased risk for workforce detachment among adults of working age, a Danish study finds. M...

item.title: Assisted Dying Bill Passed by Jersey Assembly
item.description: Jersey has approved assisted dying legislation, but delays to royal assent in the Isle of Man have raised constitutional concerns. Press Association

item.title: Post-holiday heart health: How to reset your cardiovascular habits
item.description: The holidays are good for the soul, but often hard on the heart. Weeks of indulgent meals, disrupted sleep, increased alcohol intake, stress, and inco...

item.title: Clues in the bloodstream reveal the role androgen receptor alterations play in metastatic prostate cancer
item.description: A new study using serial liquid biopsies to track how metastatic prostate cancer evolves under treatment pressure showed that androgen receptor (AR) a...

item.title: Why children enter puberty earlier: New study summarizes 10 years of research
item.description: A new Danish study compiles 10 years of research from one of the world's largest and most detailed puberty cohorts and points to three main conclusion...

item.title: Self-reported fine motor ability is associated with higher life satisfaction in cervical spinal cord injury
item.description: 1. In adults with cervical spinal cord injury, better self-reported fine motor ability is associated with higher life satisfaction and fewer life prob...

item.title: Safety and efficacy of novel antibody-oligonucleotide therapy for myotonic dystrophy type 1
item.description: 1. In this phase 1-2 randomized controlled trial involving adults with myotonic dystrophy type 1, del-desiran was well-tolerated in most patients 2. T...

item.title: Hundreds of American nurses choose Canada over the U.S. under Trump
item.description: More than 1,000 American nurses have successfully applied for licensure in British Columbia since April, a massive increase over prior years.

item.title: Surgeon general nominee Means questioned about vaccines, birth control and financial conflicts
item.description: During a confirmation hearing, senators asked Dr. Casey Means about her current positions and her past statements on a range of public health issues.

item.title: Here are 5 health topics Trump avoided in his SOTU speech
item.description: Despite President Trump's aim to make health care a "Republican issue" for the midterms, his speech left out scientific research, MAHA, abortion and r...

item.title: The ticking clock: How time constraints in medicine hurt patient care
item.description: We are taught to watch for conflicts of interest in the form of pharmaceutical pens and consulting checks, yet we often overlook the most pervasive co...

item.title: Informed consent for premeds: Is a medical career worth it?
item.description: Decades ago, becoming a doctor felt like the obvious choice. People asked, “Why wouldn’t you?” Today, the question has flipped: “Why would you ever do...

item.title: Informed refusal vs. denied care: a dental case study
item.description: I went in for a routine dental cleaning. It was never provided. I’ve spent my career on multiple sides of health care, independent practice, hospital-...

item.title: Elevated blood pressure at discharge from delivery hospitalization predicts earlier admission postpartum
item.description: 1. Blood pressure ≥140/90 mmHg at discharge predicts earlier postpartum admission but does not predict severity of hypertensive disease at readmission...

item.title: China and the U.S. alter foreign aid strategies
item.description: China's foreign aid strategy has shifted in the last few decades Now its model may be the one the U.S. is adopting even as China moves away from it.

item.title: Physicians slightly more likely to die at home or hospice
item.description: 1. In this cross-sectional study, physicians were slightly more likely to die at home or in hospice than both the general population and individuals i...
